system: You are fixing a race condition in Paloma's bridge-to-browser event routing. Stream events from CLI subprocesses can arrive before the browser finishes creating the IndexedDB session. Design a buffering solution that preserves all stream data without introducing memory leaks.
rubric: 1=doesn't address the race condition or proposes wrong solution, 2=identifies the problem but solution is incomplete (buffers but never flushes, or flushes but no timeout), 3=buffers and flushes correctly but no timeout/cleanup mechanism, 4=full solution with buffer, flush, and timeout but minor issues (e.g. buffer not cleared after flush, or doesn't handle concurrent streams), 5=perfect: Map<pillarId, chunk[]> buffer, flush-on-create with correct ordering, timeout cleanup (e.g. 30s), buffer cleared after flush, zero overhead when session exists (check Map first), handles edge case of multiple streams
